jokes.text: I love that bow. I am nuts about that bow. It humbles you. And we've got so many cocky people in this country. They need the humility of the bow here.
jokes.setup: Simple observation about Japanese bowing custom
jokes.punchline: Larry turning it into social commentary about American arrogance
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry social observation with his characteristic certainty about society's flaws

jokes.text: We're looking for a big penis... With some big balls...
jokes.setup: Discussion about art sculptures at a gallery
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt description in front of Japanese guests
jokes.explanation: Standard Larry inappropriate comment, relies on shock value more than wit

jokes.text: 'Cofey was here'. What kind of moron is this Cofey, carving his nick... Takes a knife out and carves into the table?
jokes.setup: Larry discovering graffiti carved in restaurant table
jokes.punchline: His outrage at the vandalism (sets up later reveal)
jokes.explanation: Sets up major callback payoff later - Larry's moral indignation becomes ironic

jokes.text: A kamikaze pilot. - Wow. [beat] Wouldn't he be dead?
jokes.setup: Learning Yoshi's father was a kamikaze pilot
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical but tactless question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - his literal-mindedness creates social disaster. Economical and character-perfect

jokes.text: Was he coming down, all of a sudden, he said, 'Jesus, this kamikaze business might not be for me'. 'I think maybe I'll... go back to base'?
jokes.setup: Larry theorizing what happened during the kamikaze mission
jokes.punchline: His casual speculation about the pilot's last-second change of heart
jokes.explanation: Larry's casual tone describing a life-or-death moment is perfectly inappropriate and hilarious

jokes.text: Yoshi's defeated 'I'm uncertain' response to Larry's kamikaze interrogation
jokes.setup: Larry's relentless questioning about embarrassing family history
jokes.punchline: Complete conversational shutdown - Yoshi gives up
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat. The defeat in those two words captures the exhaustion of dealing with Larry's social cluelessness. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Abrupt topic change to discussing rainbow roll sushi immediately after kamikaze awkwardness
jokes.setup: Uncomfortable silence after Yoshi's shutdown
jokes.punchline: Larry's oblivious pivot to casual food talk
jokes.explanation: Classic Curb structure - the awkwardness IS the joke. Larry's complete inability to read the room creates perfect cringe comedy through juxtaposition.

jokes.text: Bingo is the only thing in the world that I feel any sense of optimism about. When I play I actually think I'm gonna win. Isn't that insane?
jokes.setup: Discussion about going to play bingo at nursing home
jokes.punchline: Larry's rare admission of hope, specifically about bingo
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Larry finding his one source of hope in the most mundane activity

jokes.text: Well, you know, we're here. I had to really finagle to get out tonight. I'm kinda happy to be anywhere. And you know, we can't leave the house. We don't have keys. And a pizza is coming.
jokes.setup: Question of what to do after learning about suicide attempt
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd rationalizations for staying and playing poker
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of self-serving logic in the face of tragedy - darkly hilarious

jokes.text: And he tried, he didn't actually kill himself, he just tried. That's a very good distinction. Because if he said he tried to... That means he didn't, which means he's alive.
jokes.setup: Rationalizing staying to play poker
jokes.punchline: Larry's twisted logic about suicide attempts
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark - Larry using semantic analysis to justify his selfishness

jokes.text: Mayuki's fury at finding them still playing poker when she returns from hospital
jokes.setup: The group's decision to continue playing
jokes.punchline: Brutal reality check from someone who actually cares about Yoshi
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff for their selfishness. The contrast between their casual attitude and her genuine distress creates powerful cringe comedy.

jokes.text: I was up about $400. - Out! - Can we settle up? - Out! Pizza was coming, it would have been rude to leave a pizza guy...
jokes.setup: Being thrown out of the house
jokes.punchline: Still trying to collect winnings and justify staying for pizza
jokes.explanation: Perfect continuation of selfish priorities in face of tragedy

jokes.text: How do you know? - He sent me a suicide email. - A suicide email?!
jokes.setup: Learning Yoshi is okay
jokes.punchline: The modern concept of a 'suicide email'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant modernization of the suicide note concept - very quotable

jokes.text: Larry's outrage at being excluded from the suicide email cc list
jokes.setup: Learning others received the suicide email
jokes.punchline: Larry treating suicide notifications like office memos
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Larry moment. The idea of being offended at email etiquette around suicide is pure genius. Perfect character integration - only Larry would be upset about cc protocol during a crisis.

jokes.text: When you get married, you not only get the sex, but you get all the other stuff too. All the information from the wife who doesn't hold any secrets.
jokes.setup: Demanding to see the suicide email
jokes.punchline: Larry's transactional view of marriage
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's entitled view of marriage

jokes.text: Bingo scene with Larry's confident 'Bingo!' followed by immediate rejection
jokes.setup: Larry's earlier boast about his bingo winning percentage
jokes.punchline: Immediate deflation when his win is disputed
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff structure. Larry's confidence immediately undermined creates solid comedy, though the concept isn't particularly original.

jokes.text: G-41. - Not in my purview. Not in my purview.
jokes.setup: Bingo number being called
jokes.punchline: Larry using formal language to say he doesn't have the number
jokes.explanation: Characteristically pompous Larry language in mundane setting

jokes.text: Cheat. - You called I-17. Let me see that pad. - No, you're not gonna see this pad. - Why? Let me take a look at it!
jokes.setup: Dispute over bingo numbers
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately calling her a cheat and demanding evidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry escalation - going nuclear over a dollar bingo game

jokes.text: Ruthie gets a dollar! - Ha-ha, I won a dollar! I won, everybody! I won!
jokes.setup: Someone else wins immediately after Larry's controversy
jokes.punchline: Ruthie's over-the-top celebration of winning one dollar
jokes.explanation: Good contrast showing the high stakes these seniors place on small wins

jokes.text: Ruth winning immediately after Larry's disputed game, proving his conspiracy theory
jokes.setup: Larry's claims about bingo fixing
jokes.punchline: Immediate validation when Lenore's friend wins the very next game
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - the immediate validation of Larry's conspiracy theory is perfectly timed. Makes his paranoia seem justified.

jokes.text: I had bingo. You all know I had bingo. And I won your dollar.
jokes.setup: Larry still protesting the bingo result
jokes.punchline: His continued fixation on the rigged game
jokes.explanation: Larry's inability to let go of perceived injustice, even over a dollar

jokes.text: We thought of it between hands.
jokes.setup: Accused of not thinking about Yoshi's suicide attempt
jokes.punchline: Larry's minimal admission to thinking about it
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of horrible priorities - economical and devastating

jokes.text: I'm curious about how you can be a kamikaze pilot and still be alive. There's a lot of kamikaze pilots that are still alive. You say that, and I say to you, they are not kamikaze pilots.
jokes.setup: Arguing about Larry's questioning of Yoshi's father
jokes.punchline: Larry's pedantic logic about kamikaze pilot definitions
jokes.explanation: Larry doubling down on his logical but insensitive position

jokes.text: 'He grazed the ship'. That sounds to me like 'I think I might head home. This is fucking insane'.
jokes.setup: Continuing the kamikaze argument
jokes.punchline: Larry's crude interpretation of the father's war experience
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that escalates the original insensitive comment

jokes.text: I didn't call him chicken, I ordered chicken! Here's the difference: 'You're a chicken'. 'I'll have some chicken'. 'You're a chicken'. 'I'd like a piece of chicken'.
jokes.setup: Being accused of calling Yoshi chicken
jokes.punchline: Larry's pedantic demonstration of the difference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Larry's literal-mindedness missing the obvious implication

jokes.text: You're eating pistachio nuts while you're apologizing to me? - Yes, so? - You can't be sincere about apologizing and then snack on pistachio nuts!
jokes.setup: Larry trying to apologize to Yoshi on the phone
jokes.punchline: Yoshi calling out Larry for eating during the apology
jokes.explanation: Perfect observation about the incompatibility of snacking and sincere apologies

jokes.text: I've snacked and apologized many times in the past and everyone's always accepted it quite graciously.
jokes.setup: Defending his pistachio eating during apology
jokes.punchline: Larry's confident assertion about his apology track record
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Larry's obliviousness to social norms

jokes.text: She called my number. I listen, I pay attention. I'm a good bingo player.
jokes.setup: Explaining the bingo conspiracy to the doctor
jokes.punchline: Larry's pride in being a 'good bingo player'
jokes.explanation: Larry taking pride in the most basic skill imaginable

jokes.text: I think Lenore... is either in cahoots with Ruth or maybe even a few other people. I don't even know.
jokes.setup: Explaining his bingo conspiracy theory
jokes.punchline: Larry imagining a complex elderly bingo crime ring
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Larry's paranoia to absurd levels

jokes.text: You want me to go undercover to play bingo? Is that what you're saying?
jokes.setup: Larry suggesting the doctor investigate the bingo game
jokes.punchline: Doctor's incredulous response to going 'undercover' at bingo
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction to Larry's absurd request

jokes.text: Doctor vs Pharmacist standoff over medication preference
jokes.setup: Conflicting medical advice between doctor and pharmacist
jokes.punchline: Larry stuck between two medical authorities having a turf war
jokes.explanation: Good structural comedy with the back-and-forth authority conflict. Larry's position as unwilling mediator between medical egos works well.

jokes.text: I'll go with the pharmacist. I'm going with the pharmacist.
jokes.setup: The doctor vs pharmacist debate
jokes.punchline: Larry's decisive choice of the lower-ranked professional
jokes.explanation: Characteristic Larry contrarianism, choosing the underdog

jokes.text: Larry walking in on his father watching loud pornography
jokes.setup: Larry visiting his father at the nursing home
jokes.punchline: Extremely loud porn audio that can be heard in the lobby
jokes.explanation: Standard shock humor. Gets a reaction but relies on crude content rather than clever writing. The setup is competent but unoriginal.

jokes.text: You... I can hear it in the lobby, this thing! - So what? I can't hear, you know? - It's too loud! - It's very hard for me. I can't hear.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about the loud porn
jokes.punchline: His father's hearing excuse for the volume
jokes.explanation: Good excuse-making that's both logical and inappropriate

jokes.text: the Japanese guy, his son tried to kill himself. But he's home from the hospital, he didn't die. - Oh yeah, good guy... - No no, 'die'. - Lie? - No, 'die'. Die, die, die!
jokes.setup: Nat telling Ruthie about Yoshi's suicide attempt
jokes.punchline: Ruthie's hearing problems causing confusion about 'die' vs 'lie'
jokes.explanation: Standard elderly hearing joke with morbid twist

jokes.text: Is that your name? Cofey? - Yeah. - Have you ever been to that Japanese restaurant across the street? - Oh yeah, eat there all the time. - So Cofey was there. - You could say that.
jokes.setup: Larry discovering the pharmacist is named Cofey
jokes.punchline: Realizing Cofey carved his name in the table Larry complained about
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that recontextualizes Larry's earlier moral outrage

jokes.text: You know what, Cofey? I think I'll go with the doctor.
jokes.setup: Learning Cofey is the table vandal
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately switching back to the doctor's prescription
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Larry's petty moral consistency

jokes.text: Larry's BlackBerry obsession complaint while Cheryl is on her BlackBerry
jokes.setup: Larry criticizing modern technology addiction
jokes.punchline: The complaint delivered while the behavior is happening
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about early smartphone addiction. The timing works well but the concept isn't particularly original for the era.

jokes.text: Restaurant staff chanting 'Chicken teriyaki boy!' as Larry leaves
jokes.setup: Larry's earlier claims about his restaurant nickname
jokes.punchline: Validation that he actually does have this embarrassing nickname
jokes.explanation: Good callback that validates Larry's earlier claims while highlighting the misunderstanding that caused Yoshi's crisis. Solid structure.

jokes.text: I'm chicken teriyaki boy. That's my nickname, 'cause I always order chicken teriyaki. So whenever I go in, they go, 'oh, chicken teriyaki boy'! And they call you that. - Yes, that's my name.
jokes.setup: Explaining his nickname to resolve the controversy
jokes.punchline: Larry's overly detailed explanation of something obvious
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry over-explanation, but resolves plot point

jokes.text: Let's go double or nothing. Bingo. - You wanna take me on in bingo? - Tomorrow, the nursing home, 2:00.
jokes.setup: Settling their dispute over the apology
jokes.punchline: Challenging each other to a bingo game
jokes.explanation: Great escalation turning elderly bingo into a high-stakes showdown

jokes.text: Double or nothing bingo challenge between Larry and Mayuki
jokes.setup: Ongoing dispute about Larry's behavior and owed money
jokes.punchline: Settling serious emotional conflict through nursing home bingo
jokes.explanation: Good escalation bringing together multiple plot threads. Using bingo to resolve serious interpersonal conflict is appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: Oh no! Suicide blackberry.
jokes.setup: Cheryl's BlackBerry making noise
jokes.punchline: Larry connecting it to Yoshi's suicide email
jokes.explanation: Good callback that creates dark humor from technology

jokes.text: 'Suicide blackberry' - Cheryl's phone reveals bad news
jokes.setup: Cheryl receiving concerning text message
jokes.punchline: Larry connecting technology to tragedy through wordplay
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting modern technology to the episode's suicide theme. Economy is excellent but relies somewhat on dark humor for shock value.

jokes.text: Nobody beats me at bingo. Nobody! - Really? I haven't read about that.
jokes.setup: Larry boasting about his bingo prowess
jokes.punchline: Mayuki's dry response about not reading bingo news
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan deflation of Larry's absurd boasting

jokes.text: That's him. He's the one that tried to kill Ruthie. - Yes. - What? What?
jokes.setup: Larry winning bingo
jokes.punchline: Residents thinking he tried to murder Ruthie
jokes.explanation: Good escalation turning the hearing confusion into murder accusation

jokes.text: Larry's desperate explanation: 'I was saying die, die!' while yelling to help his deaf father
jokes.setup: Being accused of threatening Ruth
jokes.punchline: Larry's explanation making him sound more threatening while trying to clear his name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation. Larry's attempt to explain himself makes him sound more guilty. The irony of trying to clear his name while repeatedly shouting 'die' is perfect.

jokes.text: Larry's final 'Banzaii!' kamikaze battle cry as he's dragged away
jokes.setup: Being forcibly removed from the nursing home
jokes.punchline: Larry embracing the kamikaze theme that started his troubles
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode-ending callback. Larry's kamikaze cry brings the episode full circle while showing he's learned nothing. The structure and character integration are exceptional - this is peak Curb.
